output.code:
# Load the provided text file to analyze
path = '/mnt/data/file-P4QNifSB1fbcqteC7y5HWA-text_to_analyse.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:5000]

import re

text = open('/mnt/data/file-P4QNifSB1fbcqteC7y5HWA-text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()

# Find all curly brace spans; handle unmatched at end
spans = []
stack = []
for i, ch in enumerate(text):
    if ch == '{':
        stack.append(i)
    elif ch == '}':
        if stack:
            start = stack.pop(0)  # the earliest unmatched
            spans.append((start, i+1))  # include braces
# handle any remaining unmatched start
for start in stack:
    spans.append((start, len(text)))

# Extract content inside braces excluding braces
brace_spans = []
for s,e in spans:
    content = text[s+1:e-1] if text[e-1:e]=='}' else text[s+1:e]
    brace_spans.append((s+1, (e-1 if text[e-1:e]=='}' else e), content))

len(brace_spans), brace_spans[:5]

# find quoted segments in double quotes
quote_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text):
    quote_spans.append((m.start(1), m.end(1), m.group(1)))
quote_spans

queries:
الملائكة المذكرون في القرآن الكريم هم عددٌ قليل منهم، وهذه بعضهم:
1. **جبريل**: هو الملائكة الذي أرسل الله تعالى إلى النبي محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم ببعثته، وشرح القرآن له، ووعد به النبوة، وهداه إلى الصواب. قال الله تعالى: {فَبَشِّرْ أَهْلَ الْقُرَىٰ أَنَّهُ أَتَاكُم بِالْعِزَّةِ وَبِالْفَخَرِ لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ} [الفرقان: 1]. وقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم: "جبريل يأتيني في كل ليلة من ليلتك حتى يأتي رمضان، فيقول: أَقبَلَ رمضان، أَقبَلَ رمضان" (رواه البخاري).
من كان عدوا لله وملائكته ورسله وجبريل وميكال
وإذ قلنا للملائكة اسجدوا لآدم فسجدوا إلا إبليس
والذين كذبوا بآياتنا صم وبكم في الظلمات
جبريل يأتيني في كل ليلة من ليلتك حتى يأتي رمضان، فيقول: أَقبَلَ رمضان، أَقبَلَ رمضان
جبريل يأتيني كل ليلة حتى يأتي رمضان
Ramadan Jibril comes to me every night hadith Bukhari
Gabriel comes to me every night until Ramadan hadith

output.code:
import re, json, textwrap

text = open('/mnt/data/file-P4QNifSB1fbcqteC7y5HWA-text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()

# previously computed spans
brace_spans = [(222,330,'فَبَشِّرْ أَهْلَ الْقُرَىٰ أَنَّهُ أَتَاكُم بِالْعِزَّةِ وَبِالْفَخَرِ لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ'),
 (577,657,'وَمِيكَالَ الْمَلَكُ أَنْزَلْنَاهُ عَلَيْكَ يَحْمِلُكَ إِلَىٰ مَعْقِدِ الطَّلَعِ'),
 (766,825,'وَيُبَشِّرُ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَبِرَحْمَةٍ مُحْشِيَةٍ'),
 (932,1078,'وَمَا مِثْلُ هَذِهِ الْجَنَّةِ قُرُّ مِنْ زَيْتٍ أَوْ مِثْلُهَا مَدِينَةُ الْحِجْرِ أَوْ أَمْثَالُهَا أُخَرَ بَلْ هِيَ تَفَاوُتٌ مِنْ كُلِّ ذَلِكَ'),
 (1185,1244,'وَيُبَشِّرُ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَبِرَحْمَةٍ مُحْشِيَةٍ'),
 (1362,1455,'وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِإِبْلِيسَ وَإِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِ عُبْراً'),
 (1562,1640,'وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِبَنِي آدَمَ وَكَفىٰ بِهِمْ وُقُوفًا'),
 (1746,1830,'وَمَن يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ بَعْدَهُ وَتَرَاهُمْ إِذْ يُحْسِنُونَ'),
 (1962,2010,'وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِنْكُمْ وَأ')]

# helper to find bracket info after each span
def find_bracket_info(start_idx, end_idx, text):
    # Look for pattern \[.*?\] after end_idx within same sentence or line
    # We'll search upto next newline or full stop
    substring = text[end_idx: end_idx+100]  # 100 chars after
    m = re.search(r'\[([^\[\]]+)\]', substring)
    if not m:
        return None
    content = m.group(1).strip()
    # Expect format like "الفرقان: 1" or "البقرة: 98"
    # Remove spaces around colon
    parts = [p.strip() for p in content.split(':')]
    if len(parts) == 2:
        sura = parts[0]
        try:
            aya_num = int(re.sub(r'[^\d]', '', parts[1]))
        except:
            aya_num = None
        return {'sura_name': sura, 'aya_number': aya_num}
    else:
        return {'sura_name': content, 'aya_number': None}

# Known mapping for Sura numbers (limited ones present)
sura_number_map = {'الفرقان':25, 'البقرة':2, 'الأنعام':6}

# Extract hadith quote
hadith_spans = [(382,466,'جبريل يأتيني في كل ليلة من ليلتك حتى يأتي رمضان، فيقول: أَقبَلَ رمضان، أَقبَلَ رمضان')]

citations = []
# process Ayah-like
for s,e,content in brace_spans:
    info = find_bracket_info(s,e,text)
    sura_name = info['sura_name'] if info else None
    aya_number = info['aya_number'] if info else None
    sura_number = sura_number_map.get(sura_name, None) if sura_name else None
    citations.append({
        'span_start': s,
        'span_end': e,
        'span_text': content,
        'citation_type': 'Aya',
        'is_valid': False,
        'aya_reference': {'sura_name': sura_name, 'sura_number': sura_number, 'aya_number': aya_number} if sura_name or aya_number else {},
        'hadeeth_reference': {},
        'corrected_text': 'خطأ',
        'completely_invalid': True
    })

# process Hadith
for s,e,content in hadith_spans:
    citations.append({
        'span_start': s,
        'span_end': e,
        'span_text': content,
        'citation_type': 'Hadeeth',
        'is_valid': False,
        'aya_reference': {},
        'hadeeth_reference': {},
        'corrected_text': 'خطأ',
        'completely_invalid': True
    })

# Sort by span_start
citations_sorted = sorted(citations, key=lambda x: x['span_start'])
len(citations_sorted), citations_sorted[:2]

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
